title: Chicken Karahi

seo.title: Authentic Chicken Karahi Recipe | Easy Pakistani Dish
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Pakistani cooking with our authentic Chicken Karahi recipe! Learn how to make this flavorful, restaurant-style dish at home with easy step-by-step instructions. Perfect for a delicious meal.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant and flavorful bowl of authentic Pakistani Chicken Karahi, garnished with ginger and cilantro, ready to be served.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the rich flavors of Pakistan with this homemade Chicken Karahi, a spicy and aromatic chicken curry perfect for any occasion.

description: Chicken Karahi is a quintessential Pakistani dish known for its rich, spicy, and tangy flavors, traditionally cooked in a wok-like pan called a karahi. This beloved curry features tender chicken pieces simmered in a robust tomato-based gravy with fresh ginger, garlic, and a blend of aromatic spices. Its quick preparation and incredible taste make it a staple for family dinners and special occasions across Pakistan.

instructions.title: Sear the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat oil in a large karahi or wok over high heat. Add the chicken pieces and fry until they are lightly browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes. Remove the chicken and set aside, leaving the oil in the karahi.

instructions.title: Add Aromatics and Tomatoes
instructions.text: In the same oil, add the ginger-garlic paste and fry for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the chopped tomatoes and cook, stirring frequently, until they soften and the oil separates, about 8-10 minutes. Mash the tomatoes with the back of your spoon as they cook.

instructions.title: Introduce Spices
instructions.text: Return the seared chicken to the karahi. Add coriander powder, cumin powder, red chili powder, turmeric powder, and salt. Mix well to coat the chicken with the spices. Continue to cook for another 3-4 minutes, stirring constantly.

instructions.title: Incorporate Green Chilies and Yogurt
instructions.text: Stir in the slit green chilies. Reduce the heat to medium-low. Add the whisked yogurt and mix thoroughly. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the yogurt is fully incorporated and the gravy thickens, and oil begins to separate from the sauce, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Simmer to Perfection
instructions.text: Cover the karahi and let the chicken simmer on low heat for 10-15 minutes, or until the chicken is tender and fully cooked through. Stir once or twice during this time to prevent sticking. If the gravy becomes too thick, you can add a splash of water.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Uncover the karahi. Stir in the garam masala and freshly ground black pepper. Garnish generously with julienned fresh ginger and chopped fresh cilantro. Serve hot with naan, roti, or rice.

tips: For a truly authentic Karahi, use bone-in chicken as the bones add a lot of flavor to the gravy.
Adjust the amount of green chilies and red chili powder to your preferred spice level. For a milder dish, deseed the green chilies.
Always serve Chicken Karahi immediately after cooking with warm naan, roti, or paratha to best enjoy its fresh, vibrant flavors.
